title: Pillowy Argentinian Bread Empanadas
description: A malty and cozy Argentinian dish featuring strong white bread flour, these empanadas are diabetic-friendly and perfect for a snack or light meal.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the strong white bread flour, salt, sugar, and active dry yeast.
Gradually add the warm water to the dry ingredients and mix until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with a damp cloth, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Punch down the dough and divide it into 8 equal pieces.
Roll out each piece into a thin circle and place a tablespoon of filling in the center.
Fold the dough over the filling to form a half-moon shape and press the edges together to seal the empanada.
Brush the tops of the empanadas with the beaten egg for a golden glaze.
Bake the empanadas for 20 minutes until they are golden brown.
